storyBeat: 鼠鹿看見對岸水果 — 怎麼過河?

questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: Tonight's story has a few new words. Let's learn them first!
questions.sentenceZh: 今晚的故事會用到幾個新字，我們先把它們學會！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：今晚要講鼠鹿的故事，先來認識幾個關鍵字，記住了故事更好聽喔！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese word to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
這四個字都在鼠鹿的故事裡：
🔑 deer = 鹿
🔑 river = 河
🔑 fruit = 水果
🔑 forest = 森林

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Four more words — can you match them all?
questions.explanationZh:
再來四個：
🔑 small = 小
🔑 smart = 聰明
🔑 jump = 跳
🔑 other side = 對岸

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one shows a river?
questions.question: Which one shows a river?
questions.explanationZh: river = 河 🌊！故事裡鼠鹿站在一條寬寬的河邊，想要過去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one shows fruit?
questions.question: Which one shows fruit?
questions.explanationZh: fruit = 水果 🍎！故事裡對岸的樹上掛滿了又甜又熟的水果。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: A small deer stood by the river and looked at the fruit.
questions.sentenceZh: 一隻小鹿站在河邊看著水果。
questions.question: Which picture matches the sentence?
options: a deer running fast through a tall forest
a deer sitting beside a tree eating grass
a deer standing at a river and looking across
a deer sleeping under a big fruit tree
optionsZh: 一隻鹿在高高的森林裡快跑
一隻鹿站在河邊往對岸看
一隻鹿在大果樹下睡覺
questions.explanationZh: 句子說小鹿站在河邊、望向對岸——圖中正好是一隻鹿站在河邊往對岸看。

storyBeat: 河裡有很多鱷魚 — 該怎麼辦?

questions.speaker: grandma
questions.sentence: A few more words to learn — then the story begins!
questions.sentenceZh: 再記幾個字，故事就要開始了！
questions.explanationZh: 奶奶說：再學幾個重要的字，學會了，故事就更好懂囉！

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match each Chinese phrase to its English.
questions.explanationZh:
這四個片語都出現在鼠鹿的故事裡：
🔑 wide river = 寬河
🔑 swim across = 游過去
🔑 count them = 數一數
🔑 go back = 回去

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The river was very wide. Mouse deer could not swim across.
questions.sentenceZh: 河很寬，鼠鹿游不過去。
questions.question: Which picture matches the sentence?
options: a small deer jumping over a narrow stream
a small deer standing at a wide river it cannot cross
a small deer swimming to the middle of a lake
a small deer resting next to a dry rocky path
optionsZh: 小鹿跳過一條窄窄的小溪
小鹿站在寬河邊，過不去
小鹿在乾石頭路旁休息
questions.explanationZh: wide river = 寬河，游不過去——圖中小鹿站在寬河邊過不去，最符合。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Which one shows a crocodile?
questions.question: Which one shows a crocodile?
questions.explanationZh: crocodile = 鱷魚 🐊！故事裡河水很暗，很多鱷魚藏在水裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Mouse deer ___ a fruit tree on the other side of the river.
questions.sentenceEasy: Mouse deer ___ a fruit tree across the water.
questions.sentenceHard: Standing at the river bank, mouse deer ___ a tall fruit tree on the other side.
questions.question: Which word is correct?
questions.explanationZh:
這個句子講的是故事裡發生過的事，用過去式 saw（see 的過去式，不規則動詞）。
完整句：Mouse deer saw a fruit tree on the other side of the river.

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Let's review all the new words from this lesson.
questions.explanationZh:
把這節的重點字再複習一遍：
🔑 wide = 寬
🔑 crocodile = 鱷魚
🔑 count = 數
🔑 swim = 游泳

storyBeat: 鼠鹿想到聰明的辦法 — 他會說什麼謊?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 think = 想
🔑 plan = 計畫
🔑 smile = 笑容
🔑 king = 國王
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil sat down. He thought hard for a long time.
questions.explanationZh: Sang Kancil 坐下來。他用力想了好久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His head moved slowly from one side to the other side, again and again.
questions.explanationZh: 頭一直左右搖——他在用力想,當然是 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的腦袋慢慢左右轉，一下左，一下右，反覆想著。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then a big smile came on his face. He had a plan.
questions.explanationZh: 然後他的臉上出現大大的笑容。他想到計畫了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His small eyes turned bright. His tail started to move quick.
questions.question: What did the smile mean?
questions.explanationZh: 眼睛亮 + 尾巴動 → 想到辦法。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的小眼睛突然亮起來，尾巴也快速搖了起來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I cannot swim, but I do not need to swim," he said softly.
questions.explanationZh: 「我不會游泳,但我不需要游泳,」他小聲說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How was mouse deer going to cross?
questions.question: How would he cross the river?
questions.explanationZh: 不游泳卻能過 → 用聰明的計謀。
questions.sentenceZh: 小鹿打算怎麼過河？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He walked to the side of the river. He called out loud.
questions.explanationZh: 他走到河邊。他大聲喊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His small voice rang out over the dark water of the river.
questions.question: How loud did mouse deer call?
questions.explanationZh: 聲音響徹整條河 → 很大聲。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的小聲音響過那片黑漆漆的河面。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Mouse deer was about to tell the crocodiles something that was not true.
questions.question: Why was mouse deer going to lie?
options: he liked telling lies for fun
he was bored with nothing to do
he needed a way to cross safely
questions.explanationZh: 游不過去、河裡到處是鱷魚——他需要安全過河,才想到說謊這個辦法。
questions.sentenceZh: 小鹿準備跟鱷魚說一件不是真的事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil sat down. He thought hard for a long time.
questions.question: Why did mouse deer sit down?
options: needed to think of a plan
wanted to rest his short legs
questions.explanationZh: 坐下用力想——他需要想出一個辦法過河。
questions.sentenceZh: Sang Kancil 坐了下來，用力想了很久。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His head moved slowly from one side to the other side, again and again.
questions.questionEn: Did the plan come to mouse deer quickly?
questions.explanationZh: 頭反覆慢慢搖——花了很長時間,所以不是一下子就想到,答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的腦袋慢慢左右搖，一次又一次。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Then a big smile came on his face. He had a plan.
questions.question: What did the big smile show?
questions.explanationZh: 大大的笑容 + 想到計畫——笑容代表「想到好主意了」。
questions.sentenceZh: 然後他臉上浮現大大的笑容——他想到辦法了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the phrases.
questions.explanationZh: 配對:big smile=大笑容、smart plan=聰明計畫、bright eyes=亮眼睛、call out loud=大聲喊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "I cannot swim, but I do not need to swim," he said softly.
questions.question: What does this line tell us?
options: he learned to swim just now
optionsZh: 想到不需要游泳的辦法
questions.explanationZh: 不需要游泳——他想到了另一個不用游泳的辦法。
questions.sentenceZh: 「我不會游泳，但我不需要游泳，」他小聲說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He walked to the side of the river. He called out loud.
questions.questionEn: Did mouse deer stay far away from the river?
questions.explanationZh: 他走到河邊——靠近了河,不是待在遠處,答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠走到河邊，大聲喊了出去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Mouse deer was about to tell the crocodiles something that was not true.
questions.question: What was mouse deer planning to do?
options: trick the crocodiles with a lie
questions.explanationZh: 要說不是真的事情——就是用謊話騙鱷魚。
questions.sentenceZh: 小鹿準備跟鱷魚說一件不是真的事。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil sat down. He thought hard for a long time. Then a big smile came on his face.
questions.question: What is the main idea of this lesson?
options: small mouse deer finds a clever plan
mouse deer gives up and goes home
king sends a message to the animals
optionsZh: 小鼠鹿想到聰明的辦法
questions.explanationZh: 坐下苦思,笑容一出——小鼠鹿找到了聰明的計畫。
questions.sentenceZh: Sang Kancil 坐下苦思，然後臉上浮現了大大的笑容。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Hello, crocodiles! I have a message from the king!"
questions.explanationZh: 「嗨,鱷魚們!我從國王那邊帶了話來!」

storyBeat: 對鱷魚說「國王要點數量」 — 鱷魚相信嗎?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 come up = 上來
🔑 count = 數
🔑 line up = 排隊
🔑 many = 很多
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles came up from the water one by one.
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚一隻接一隻從水裡上來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their big eyes turned to look at the little mouse deer on the side.
questions.explanationZh: 眼睛都轉過來看他——鱷魚們在注意呢,答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚們把大眼睛轉向河邊那隻小鹿。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "What does the king want?" the big one asked.
questions.explanationZh: 「國王要什麼?」最大的那隻問。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big crocodile lifted his head higher than the rest of the group.
questions.question: Who asked mouse deer the question?
questions.explanationZh: 抬頭比其他高 → 最大的鱷魚。
questions.sentenceZh: 一條大鱷魚把頭抬得比其他同伴都高。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The king wants to know how many crocodiles live here," he said.
questions.explanationZh: 「國王想知道這裡住了幾隻鱷魚,」他說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Was this true?
questions.question: Was the king's message true?
questions.explanationZh: 鼠鹿自己想的計謀 → 是說謊。
questions.sentenceZh: 這是真的嗎？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles looked at each other. None of them knew about a king.
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚們你看我我看你。沒有誰知道有什麼國王。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But the big one did not want to say he did not know.
questions.question: Why did the big crocodile stay quiet?
options: he was too tired to speak
he liked the king very much
his mouth was full of food
he did not want to look bad
questions.explanationZh: 不想說「我不知道」 → 不想看起來不知道。
questions.sentenceZh: 但那條大鱷魚不想承認自己不知道。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles believed the lie because no one wanted to ask hard questions.
questions.question: Why did the crocodiles believe the lie?
options: no one wanted to look like they did not know
mouse deer was very tall and strong
the king was their very good friend
they all loved counting very much
questions.explanationZh: 沒有人想看起來「不知道」——大家都怕丟臉,就這樣相信了,選「沒人想看起來不知道」。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚們相信了那個謊，因為沒有人願意問難題。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles came up from the water one by one.
questions.question: How did the crocodiles come up?
options: all at once in a crowd
questions.explanationZh: 「one by one」→ 一條一條地出來。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚們一條一條從水裡浮了出來。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their big eyes turned to look at the little mouse deer on the side.
questions.questionEn: Were the crocodiles watching mouse deer?
questions.explanationZh: 大眼睛全轉過來看——是的,在盯著鼠鹿,答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠們的大眼睛轉過來，看著河邊的小鼠鹿。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "The king wants to know how many crocodiles live here," he said.
questions.question: What did mouse deer claim the king wanted?
options: a big fish from the river
some fruit from the other side
questions.explanationZh: 他說國王要知道這裡住了多少條鱷魚——要清點數量。
questions.sentenceZh: 「國王想知道這裡住了多少條鱷魚，」他說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the phrases.
questions.explanationZh: 配對:come up=上來、the king=國王、how many=幾條、line up=排隊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles looked at each other. None of them knew about a king.
questions.question: What did the crocodiles think about the king story?
options: knew it was all a lie
questions.explanationZh: 沒人知道有沒有國王,但也沒人開口問——不確定卻沉默了。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚們互看了一眼，但沒有一條知道有什麼國王。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But the big one did not want to say he did not know.
questions.questionEn: Did the big crocodile admit he knew nothing about a king?
questions.explanationZh: 大鱷魚不想承認他不知道——所以沒說出口,答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 但大鱷魚不想說自己不知道。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles believed the lie because no one wanted to ask hard questions.
questions.question: Why did the crocodiles fall for the trick?
options: too proud to admit not knowing
wanted to help mouse deer cross
optionsZh: 太愛面子,不肯承認不知道
國王真的是他們的主人
questions.explanationZh: 大鱷魚不想說「我不知道」——愛面子使他們上當了。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚相信了這個謊，因為沒人想問難回答的問題。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles came up from the water. They believed mouse deer's message from the king.
questions.question: What is the main idea of this lesson?
options: the king really sent mouse deer to count them
he fooled them with a clever count
crocodiles chased mouse deer away from the river
mouse deer decided to go back to the forest
optionsZh: 國王真的派鼠鹿來點人數
鼠鹿用國王的故事騙了鱷魚
鱷魚把鼠鹿從河邊趕走了
questions.explanationZh: 鼠鹿用「國王的話」這個謊言讓鱷魚相信了——這是整節的核心。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚從水裡出來，相信了鼠鹿說的國王傳話。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Line up across the river so I can count you all," he said...
questions.explanationZh: 「在河上排成一排,讓我數一數你們有幾隻,」他說……

questions.sentence: The king wants to know how many.
questions.answer: The king wants to know how many.
accept: The king wants to know the number.
The king needs to know how many.
The king wishes to know how many.
questions.explanationZh: 鼠鹿騙鱷魚說這是國王的命令。wants to know how many = 想知道幾隻。
questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentenceZh: 國王想知道幾隻。

questions.sentence: No one wanted to look bad.
questions.answer: No one wanted to look bad.
accept: None of them wanted to look bad.
No crocodile wanted to look bad.
No one wanted to seem like they did not know.
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚們都不肯開口問——look bad = 看起來很差、丟臉。
questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentenceZh: 沒有鱷魚想看起來不知道。

questions.sentence: Mouse deer told the crocodiles to line up.
questions.answer: Mouse deer told the crocodiles to line up.
accept: Mouse deer asked the crocodiles to line up.
He told the crocodiles to line up across the river.
Mouse deer said to the crocodiles: line up.
questions.explanationZh: 鼠鹿說「排成一排讓我來數」——told … to line up = 叫……排隊。這是他計謀的關鍵一步！
questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentenceZh: 鼠鹿叫鱷魚在河上排好隊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The big crocodile did not want to say he did not know about a king.
questions.question: What does the big crocodile's silence tell us about him?
options: He was too frightened to open his mouth
He cared more about his pride than the truth
He already knew the king very well
He wanted to help mouse deer cross safely
optionsZh: 他的面子比真相更重要
他早就非常了解那位國王
他想幫助鼠鹿安全過河
questions.explanationZh: 大鱷魚寧可沉默也不肯承認「我不知道」——說明面子（pride）比弄清真相更重要。這正是鼠鹿計謀成功的原因！
questions.sentenceZh: 大鱷魚不想說自己不知道有什麼國王。

storyBeat: 鱷魚排隊讓他點 — 他跳過鱷魚背

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 jump = 跳
🔑 next = 下一個
🔑 on = 在上面
🔑 back = 背
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles lined up from this side to the other side.
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚從這邊排到對岸,排成一條線。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their long bodies made a flat road across the dark water.
questions.questionEn: Did the crocodiles make a kind of bridge?
questions.explanationZh: 長長的鱷魚身體排成一排平路——那不就像一座橋嗎?答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠們長長的身體拼成一條平坦的路，橫跨過黑水。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil jumped on the first crocodile back. "One!"
questions.explanationZh: Sang Kancil 跳上第一隻鱷魚的背。「一!」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His small feet pressed soft on the wet skin of the long animal.
questions.question: How did mouse deer move?
questions.explanationZh: 腳踩在鱷魚身上 → 跳鱷魚背。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的小腳輕輕踩在那條長動物濕滑的背上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He jumped to the next. "Two!" Then "Three!" "Four!"
questions.explanationZh: 他跳到下一隻。「二!」然後「三!」「四!」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How was mouse deer counting?
questions.question: How did mouse deer count?
questions.explanationZh: 一邊跳一邊喊 → 每跳一下大聲數。
questions.sentenceZh: 小鹿是怎麼數數的？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles felt proud. They thought the king was watching.
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚們覺得很驕傲。他們以為國王在看。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They kept very still so the small mouse deer could count them well.
questions.question: Why did the crocodiles stay still?
options: they wanted to be counted right
they were too tired to move
mouse deer was too heavy to carry
they were asleep in the sun
questions.explanationZh: 不動讓他好數 → 想被好好數。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠們乖乖不動，讓小鹿好好數清楚。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every jump moved mouse deer one step closer to the fruit tree.
questions.question: What was really happening?
options: mouse deer was crossing the river
questions.explanationZh: 每跳一下就靠近對岸的果樹——鼠鹿其實是在過河,選「鼠鹿在過河」。
questions.sentenceZh: 每跳一步，小鹿就離果樹近一點點。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles lined up from this side to the other side.
questions.question: What did the crocodiles form by lining up?
options: a long wall to block the water
a path back to the forest
questions.explanationZh: 從這岸排到對岸——形成了一座活生生的橋。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚從這岸一直排到對岸。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their long bodies made a flat road across the dark water.
questions.questionEn: Did the crocodiles' bodies help mouse deer cross?
questions.explanationZh: 牠們的身體形成了一條平坦的路——確實幫助鼠鹿過河了,答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠們長長的身體在暗水上鋪成了一條平路。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil jumped on the first crocodile back. "One!"
questions.question: Why did mouse deer call out a number?
options: warning the crocodiles to stay still
shouting for joy at each hop
pretending to count for the king
questions.explanationZh: 大聲說「一!」讓鱷魚以為是在點人數——繼續扮演國王使者的角色。
questions.sentenceZh: Sang Kancil 跳上第一條鱷魚的背——「一！」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the phrases.
questions.explanationZh: 配對:lined up=排成一排、jumped on=跳上去、kept still=保持不動、felt proud=感到驕傲。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: He jumped to the next. "Two!" Then "Three!" "Four!"
questions.question: How did mouse deer move across?
questions.explanationZh: 二!三!四!——每次數一條就跳一下,一背一背地過去。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠跳到下一條——「二！」然後「三！」「四！」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles felt proud. They thought the king was watching.
questions.questionEn: Were the crocodiles angry while mouse deer counted them?
questions.explanationZh: 他們感到驕傲、覺得國王在看——心情是開心驕傲,不是生氣,答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚們感到驕傲，以為國王在看著牠們。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: They kept very still so the small mouse deer could count them well.
questions.question: Why staying still helped mouse deer secretly?
options: made each hop safe and easy
let him rest on their backs
gave him time to eat fruit
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚靜止不動 → 背部平穩 → 鼠鹿每一跳都更安全、更好跳。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠們乖乖不動，這樣小鼠鹿才能好好點人數。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Every jump moved mouse deer one step closer to the fruit tree.
questions.question: What was mouse deer really doing all along?
options: looking for a bridge further away
counting animals for the true king
crossing the river using the crocodiles
waiting for the crocodiles to fall asleep
questions.explanationZh: 每一跳都靠近對岸一點——他一直都是在借鱷魚背過河。
questions.sentenceZh: 每跳一步，鼠鹿就離果樹更近一步。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: One by one, he hopped on every long crocodile back...
questions.explanationZh: 一隻接一隻,他跳過每一條長長的鱷魚背……

storyBeat: 到對岸吃水果 — 鱷魚發現被騙

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 off = 離開
🔑 other side = 對岸
🔑 anger = 生氣
🔑 already = 已經
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil jumped off the last crocodile onto the other side.
questions.explanationZh: Sang Kancil 從最後一隻鱷魚跳下,落到對岸。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His four feet landed soft on the warm dry land of the other side.
questions.questionEn: Did mouse deer reach the other side?
questions.explanationZh: 四隻腳穩穩落在乾燥的地上——到對岸了!答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的四隻腳輕輕落在對岸溫暖的乾地上。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Thank you, crocodiles! There is no message from the king!"
questions.explanationZh: 「謝謝你們,鱷魚們!根本沒有什麼國王的話!」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The little mouse deer turned around and called back across the water.
questions.question: What did mouse deer tell the crocodiles?
questions.explanationZh: 坦白沒國王訊息 → 根本沒有國王的話。
questions.sentenceZh: 小鹿轉過身，對著河對岸大聲喊回去。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles opened their mouths in anger. They had no king.
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚們生氣地張開嘴巴。他們根本沒有國王。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did the crocodiles feel now?
questions.question: How did the crocodiles feel?
questions.explanationZh: 張嘴 + 發現被騙 → 很生氣。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚現在是什麼感覺？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But the river was too wide. They could not chase him.
questions.explanationZh: 但河太寬了。他們追不到他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Their bodies could go in the water but not up the dry land.
questions.question: Why could the crocodiles not catch mouse deer?
options: they were full from a big meal
they could not go on dry land
they were too busy playing games
they fell asleep by the river
questions.explanationZh: 只能在水裡 → 不能上乾地。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠們的身體可以在水裡，但爬不上乾岸。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: On the other side of the river, mouse deer found the fruit tree at last.
questions.question: What did mouse deer do next?
options: he said sorry to the crocs
he ate the fruit he wanted
questions.explanationZh: 他到對岸了、整個目標就是那棵果樹——當然是吃掉想吃的水果,選「他吃掉想吃的水果」。
questions.sentenceZh: 在河的對岸，小鹿終於找到了那棵果樹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil jumped off the last crocodile onto the other side.
questions.question: What did Sang Kancil do after the last crocodile?
options: said sorry to all the crocodiles
swam back to where he started
hid near the fruit tree quietly
jumped onto dry land across the river
questions.explanationZh: 跳離最後一條鱷魚,落到對岸——成功了!
questions.sentenceZh: Sang Kancil 從最後一條鱷魚身上跳到了對岸。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Thank you, crocodiles! There is no message from the king!"
questions.questionEn: Did mouse deer keep the lie a secret after crossing?
questions.explanationZh: 他大喊「根本沒有國王的話」——主動揭穿了自己的謊言,答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 「謝謝你們，鱷魚！根本沒有什麼國王的話！」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles opened their mouths in anger. They had no king.
questions.question: Why were the crocodiles so angry?
options: mouse deer took all their food
the king punished them for lining up
mouse deer broke one of their tails
optionsZh: 鼠鹿搶走了他們的食物
鼠鹿弄斷了其中一條的尾巴
questions.explanationZh: 根本沒有國王——他們被鼠鹿耍了,當然大怒。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚憤怒地張開大嘴——根本沒有什麼國王。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the phrases.
questions.explanationZh: 配對:jumped off=跳下去、in anger=生氣地、dry land=乾地、too wide=太寬了。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But the river was too wide. They could not chase him.
questions.question: Why couldn't the crocodiles catch mouse deer now?
options: mouse deer ran into a dark cave
river too wide to cross to dry land
the king ordered them to stop
they were still lining up in the water
questions.explanationZh: 寬寬的河擋住了他們——水裡的動物上不了乾燥的陸地追他。
questions.sentenceZh: 但河太寬了，牠們追不上他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: On the other side of the river, mouse deer found the fruit tree at last.
questions.questionEn: Did mouse deer reach the fruit tree in the end?
questions.explanationZh: 「at last」終於找到了果樹——成功了,答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 在河的對岸，鼠鹿終於找到了那棵果樹。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The little mouse deer turned around and called back across the water.
questions.question: Why did mouse deer call back?
options: asked them to send fruit across
needed more help from the crocodiles
wanted the crocodiles to know the trick
said sorry for what he had done
optionsZh: 還需要鱷魚再幫他一次
questions.explanationZh: 大聲宣告「沒有國王的話」——他故意讓鱷魚知道自己被耍了。
questions.sentenceZh: 小鼠鹿轉過身，朝著水的對面大喊。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil jumped off the last crocodile onto the other side. The crocodiles could not chase him.
questions.question: What is the main point of this lesson?
options: mouse deer swam the last part on his own
crocodiles chased mouse deer all the way to the tree
the king arrived and stopped the crocodiles from chasing
mouse deer crossed safely and outsmarted the crocodiles
optionsZh: 鼠鹿最後一段自己游過去的
鼠鹿安全過河並耍了鱷魚
questions.explanationZh: 鼠鹿成功到達對岸,揭穿謊言,鱷魚也追不上——這就是這節的重點。
questions.sentenceZh: Sang Kancil 跳上對岸，鱷魚們追不上他。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: But Sang Kancil was already eating sweet fruit under the tree...
questions.explanationZh: 但 Sang Kancil 已經在樹下吃又甜又熟的水果了……

storyBeat: 鼠鹿學到什麼? 鱷魚學到什麼?

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Here are 4 words you will meet in tonight's story.
questions.explanationZh:
本節新單字 (左中右英):
🔑 belly = 肚子
🔑 fine = 沒問題
🔑 better = 更好
🔑 believe = 相信
背熟這 4 個字,故事就會輕鬆聽懂。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil ate the fruit. He patted his small full belly.
questions.explanationZh: Sang Kancil 吃完水果。他拍拍自己飽飽的小肚子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His face was bright and his tail moved fast from side to side.
questions.explanationZh: 臉亮亮的、尾巴搖來搖去——這不是很開心是什麼?答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的臉亮亮的，尾巴左右搖個不停。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "A small body is fine. A smart head is better," he said.
questions.explanationZh: 「小小的身體沒問題。聰明的腦袋更好,」他說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His own voice sounded soft and slow, like a teacher in the woods.
questions.question: What lesson did mouse deer learn?
options: fruit is not good to eat
questions.explanationZh: 小身體 + 聰明腦袋 → 會想就能贏。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠自己的聲音聽起來輕輕柔柔，像森林裡在上課的老師。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles slowly went back into the dark water.
questions.explanationZh: 鱷魚們慢慢沉回黑色的水裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: How did the crocodiles look now?
questions.question: How did the crocodiles feel?
questions.explanationZh: 慢慢沉回水裡 → 安靜又不好意思。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚現在看起來怎麼樣？

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Next time we must ask more questions," the big crocodile said.
questions.explanationZh: 「下次我們得多問問題,」最大的鱷魚說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His low voice came up from the dark water in a slow sad sound.
questions.question: How did the big crocodile sound?
questions.explanationZh: 低低慢慢悲悲的 → 低又慢。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠低沉的聲音從黑水裡慢慢飄上來，帶著點悲傷。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles learned to ask "is this true?" before saying yes.
questions.question: What did the crocodiles learn?
options: check if a story is true
optionsZh: 先想想故事是不是真的
questions.explanationZh: 被騙過之後,學到「要多問問題」——也就是先想想那個故事是不是真的。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚們學到了：要先問「這是真的嗎？」再點頭答應。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil ate the fruit. He patted his small full belly.
questions.question: How did mouse deer feel after eating the fruit?
options: ready to go back and say sorry
questions.explanationZh: 拍拍飽飽的小肚子——吃飽了,心滿意足。
questions.sentenceZh: Sang Kancil 吃了水果，拍拍他圓滾滾的小肚子。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: His face was bright and his tail moved fast from side to side.
questions.questionEn: Was mouse deer unhappy after eating the fruit?
questions.explanationZh: 臉亮亮的、尾巴搖得快——他很開心,不是不開心,答 No。
questions.sentenceZh: 牠的臉亮晶晶的，尾巴也快速左右搖。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "A small body is fine. A smart head is better," he said.
questions.question: What was mouse deer's lesson for himself?
options: a big body helps you the most
lying is always the best answer
optionsZh: 聰明比體型和力氣更重要
說謊永遠是最好的辦法
questions.explanationZh: 「小身體沒關係,聰明的腦袋更好」——智慧勝過體型。
questions.sentenceZh: 「小身子沒什麼，聰明的腦袋更厲害，」他說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Match the phrases.
questions.explanationZh: 配對:full belly=飽肚子、smart head=聰明的腦、next time=下一次、believe every=相信每一個。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles slowly went back into the dark water.
questions.question: How did the crocodiles move back into the water?
options: step by step, one at a time
questions.explanationZh: 「slowly」→ 慢慢地回到水裡——也許有點垂頭喪氣。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚們慢慢地回到暗水裡。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "Next time we must ask more questions," the big crocodile said.
questions.questionEn: Did the big crocodile want to do better next time?
questions.explanationZh: 「下次一定要多問幾個問題」——他想做得更好,答 Yes。
questions.sentenceZh: 「下次我們一定要多問幾個問題，」大鱷魚說。

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: The crocodiles learned to ask "is this true?" before saying yes.
questions.question: What lesson did the crocodiles learn the hard way?
options: the king will protect them next time
small animals cannot be trusted ever
never go up to the surface again
always check before trusting a story
optionsZh: 相信一件事之前先查清楚
questions.explanationZh: 在說「好」之前先問「這是真的嗎?」——相信之前先核查。
questions.sentenceZh: 鱷魚學到了：在相信之前，要先問「這是真的嗎？」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: Sang Kancil ate the fruit. "A small body is fine. A smart head is better."
questions.question: What is the main message of the whole story?
options: being clever is stronger than being big
you must lie to get what you want
fruit is the most important thing in life
optionsZh: 要得到想要的就得說謊
水果是生命中最重要的事
questions.explanationZh: 整個故事都在說——聰明的小鼠鹿靠智慧勝過了大鱷魚。這就是故事的主旨。
questions.sentenceZh: Sang Kancil 吃著水果，「小身子沒什麼，聰明的腦袋更厲害。」

questions.speaker: narrator
questions.sentence: "We must not believe every voice from the side of the river."
questions.explanationZh: 「我們不能相信河邊每一個傳來的聲音。」
